L1.area: Cryptographic hash construction and sponge-based hashing logic

L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists in the sponge-based hash construction where variable-length inputs are accepted without injective padding. When fewer inputs than the sponge rate are provided, unused positions are implicitly zero-filled, allowing trivial hash collisions between messages of different lengths that share a common prefix.

L3.area: Poseidon V1 sponge hash construction and its input absorption logic
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists in the Poseidon V1 sponge hash construction where variable-length inputs are accepted without injective padding. When a caller provides fewer inputs than the sponge rate, unused rate positions are implicitly zero-filled, enabling trivial collisions between input vectors of different lengths (e.g., a single-element input collides with that element followed by zero).

ground_truth.reason: PoseidonSponge accepts variable-length inputs without enforcing full-rate usage, causing unused rate positions to be implicitly zero-filled. This allows trivial hash collisions between inputs of different lengths that share a common prefix.
